name: Mitchell Summerfield
role: Pastor, Word of God Fellowship Church, Raleigh, North Carolina
incident: Mitchell Summerfield, pastor of the Word of God Fellowship Church in Raleigh, pleaded guilty on May 27, 2025 to conspiracy to commit bank fraud and wire fraud. Between July 2020 and July 2021, Summerfield submitted false and fraudulent applications for PPP loans and Economic Injury Disaster loans totaling more than $400,000 across multiple entities he controlled. He faces a maximum sentence of 30 years in prison.

name: Paul Mitchell
role: Lead Pastor and Daycare President, Brooklyn, New York
incident: Paul Mitchell, lead pastor of a Brooklyn church and president of a church-affiliated daycare, pleaded guilty on December 16, 2025 to tax evasion. Between 2015 and 2022, Mitchell used his organizations' credit cards to pay for personal expenses including men's clothing, thousands of dollars in jewelry, luxury watches, and life insurance premiums. U.S. Attorney Joseph Nocella stated Mitchell treated his organizations' accounts as a personal piggy bank, stealing millions and betraying his congregation.

name: John McKinzie
role: Lead Pastor, Hope Fellowship Church, Frisco, Texas
incident: John McKinzie, 59, lead pastor of Hope Fellowship Church in Frisco, Texas, resigned on September 14, 2025 after confessing to 'sexual sin and moral failure' during a meeting with church leadership. He was the second pastor at the same megachurch to step down over similar circumstances in 2025. Church elders stated no one on staff had any prior knowledge of the moral failure before McKinzie disclosed it.
